input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule F.8.8.8 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.8.8.8
            TITLE: test analysis

            CONTENT:
            rule F.8.8.8: Test Analysis
F.8.8.8 Test Analysis 
a. When using acceleration data from the dynamic test, the average deceleration must be 
calculated based on the raw unfiltered data.   
b. If peaks above the 40 g limit are present in the data, a Channel Filter Class (CFC) 60 
(100Hz) filter per SAE Recommended Practice J211 “Instrumentation for Impact Test”, or 
a 100 Hz, 3rd order, low pass Butterworth (-3dB at 100 Hz) filter may be applied.

final_answer: rule F.8.8.8: Test Analysis F.8.8.8 Test Analysis a. When using acceleration data from the dynamic test, the average deceleration must be calculated based on the raw unfiltered data. b. If peaks above the 40 g limit are present in the data, a Channel Filter Class (CFC) 60 (100Hz) filter per SAE Recommended Practice J211 “Instrumentation for Impact Test”, or a 100 Hz, 3rd order, low pass Butterworth (-3dB at 100 Hz) filter may be applied.
